feat: Add Communications Panel for Teachers (#3833)
prs.body:
# Pull Request: Teacher Communications Panel (#3833)

**Closes #3833**

**Title**: `feat: Add Communications Panel for Teachers (#3833)`

## Description

This PR implements a "Communications Panel" interface specifically for teachers, addressing the request in issue **#3833**. This feature empowers teachers to communicate directly with students in their assigned classes through a secure, moderated system linked from their dashboard.

### Key Features & Implementation Details
- **Scoped Recipient Filtering**: Implemented filtering logic in the new `TeacherCommModule` to ensure teachers can *only* email students enrolled in their own classes. This maintains strict data privacy and prevents accidental system-wide mailing.
- **Secure Email Dispatch**: Fully integrated with the site's **`dbmail`** and **`MessageRequest`** models. This ensures teacher-sent emails are correctly queued, asynchronized, and logged.
- **Compose & Preview Workflow**: Added a 3-step UI (Select Classes -> Compose/Preview -> Confirm) using the site's standard Bootstrap/Vanilla CSS patterns for visual consistency.
- **Dashboard Link**: Integrated a "Teacher Communications" entry in the teacher registration dashboard (`esp/templates/program/modules/teacherclassregmodule/listclasses.html`).

### Components & Files
- **Backend Handler**: `esp/esp/program/modules/handlers/teachercommmodule.py` (Standardized `ProgramModuleObj`)
- **UI Templates**: 
  - `esp/templates/program/modules/teachercommmodule/commpanel_teacher.html`
  - `esp/templates/program/modules/teachercommmodule/preview_teacher.html`
  - `esp/templates/program/modules/teachercommmodule/finished.html`
  - `esp/templates/program/modules/teachercommmodule/no_recipients.html`

### Verification & Security
- [x] All views are protected by the `@needs_teacher` decorator.
- [x] Recipient filtering logic cross-references the authenticated `request.user` with `ClassSubject.teachers`.
- [x] Verified that `PersistentQueryFilter` isolates the specific students for each teacher.

---

### How to Verify
1. Navigate to the teacher dashboard (`/teach/[program]/[instance]/`).
2. Click the new **"Open Communications Panel"** link at the bottom.
3. Select a class and compose a test message.
4. Verify that the **Preview** renders correctly and that the message is successfully queued in the system.

prs.title: fix: warn when addHttpMethod overrides an existing HTTP method
prs.body:
## Summary

Closes #6284

`addHttpMethod` could silently reconfigure built-in HTTP methods (e.g. `GET`, `POST`) without any indication to the developer. The function name implies *adding* new methods, not *overriding* existing ones.

## Changes

This PR introduces a new `overrideExisting` option (default: `false`) to `addHttpMethod`.

- When a method that **already exists** in `kSupportedHTTPMethods` is passed **without** `overrideExisting: true`, a deprecation warning (**FSTWRN005**) is emitted.
- The actual reconfiguration still proceeds — **fully non-breaking in v5**.
- In Fastify v6, calling `addHttpMethod` on an existing method without `{ overrideExisting: true }` will throw an error.

## Usage

```js
// v5 — emits FSTWRN005 deprecation warning, still works
fastify.addHttpMethod('GET', { hasBody: false })

// v5 — no warning, override is explicit
fastify.addHttpMethod('GET', { overrideExisting: true })

// Unchanged — adding a brand new method works as before
fastify.addHttpMethod('SEARCH', { hasBody: true })
```

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `lib/warnings.js` | Added `FSTWRN005` warning |
| `fastify.js` | Added `overrideExisting` option to `addHttpMethod` |
| `types/instance.d.ts` | Updated TypeScript signature |
| `test/http-methods/custom-http-methods.test.js` | 3 new tests |
| `test/types/instance.test-d.ts` | 2 new type tests |

prs.title: [FI] fix: resolve message bus data leakage
prs.body:
### Description
Fixes the critical race condition in the message bus reported in #731, ensuring that concurrent subscribers no longer share mutable state.

### Changes
- **Immutability**: Converted `OutboundMessage` to a `frozen` dataclass to prevent accidental state mutation at runtime.
- **Deep-Copy Isolation**: Refactored `MessageBus.publish_outbound` and `broadcast_outbound` to provide every subscriber with a unique `deepcopy` of `metadata` and `media` fields.
- **Improved Concurrency**: Standardized broadcasting logic to use `asyncio.gather` for true parallel execution across channels.

### Verification
- [x] **Bug Reproduction**: Confirmed the fix with a reproduction test proving that modifications in one subscriber do not leak to others.
- [x] **Regression Testing**: All existing tests in `tests/test_bus.py` passed.

Fixes #731

# [FI] feat: add GitHub integration tool

## Summary
Closes #709

This PR introduces a dedicated **`GitHubTool`** to PocketPaw, allowing the agent to interact with the GitHub ecosystem. As an AI assistant built for developers, being able to directly query repository metadata, list active issues, and retrieve detailed conversation context from pull requests is a critical enhancement.

## Changes

### 🛠️ Core Integration
- **`src/pocketpaw/integrations/github.py`**: New `GitHubClient` using `httpx` for efficient, asynchronous API communication.

### 🔐 Configuration & Security

- **`src/pocketpaw/credentials.py`**: Added `github_api_token` to `SECRET_FIELDS` to ensure it is stored encrypted on disk.

### ✅ Quality Assurance

prs.title: Forbid wildcard (*) CORS headers when credentials are true
prs.body:
### Description
This PR addresses issue #333 by ensuring that the `*` wildcard is not used in `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`, `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`, or `Access-Control-Expose-Headers` response headers when `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials` is set to `true`, as required by the [Fetch Standard](https://fetch.spec.whatwg.org/#cors-protocol-and-credentials).

### Changes
- **Origin**: If `origin: '*'` (default) is set and `credentials: true`, the middleware now reflects the `Origin` request header and adds `Vary: Origin`.
- **Methods**: If `methods: '*'` is explicitly set and `credentials: true`, the `Access-Control-Allow-Methods` header is omitted from the response.
- **Allowed Headers**: If `allowedHeaders: '*'` is explicitly set and `credentials: true`, the `Access-Control-Allow-Headers` header is omitted from the response.
- **Exposed Headers**: If `exposedHeaders: '*'` is explicitly set and `credentials: true`, the `Access-Control-Expose-Headers` header is omitted from the response.

### Verification
- Added a new test file [test/issue-333.js](file:///Users/vanshagarwal/cors/test/issue-333.js) covering all the above scenarios.
- All existing tests pass.
- Code coverage remains at 100%.

prs.title: fix(electron): resolve Ubuntu 24.04 Snap startup errors
prs.body:
This PR addresses the startup issues reported on Ubuntu 24.04 (Noble Numbat) for the Snap package. Users were experiencing GUI-related errors (missing libraries and sandbox violations) that prevented the application from launching correctly.
## Changes
### 📦 Snap Configuration (`electron-builder.yml`)
- **Updated base to `core24`**: Aligns the snap with modern Ubuntu systems.
- **Added `gnome` extension**: Automatically provides necessary desktop environment libraries and dependencies.
- **Improved Security**: Switched `confinement` to `strict` and `grade` to `stable`.
- **Cleaned Layout**: Removed the manual `/usr/share/libdrm` layout binding as it is now properly handled by the gnome extension.
### 🛠️ Build Configuration ([electron.vite.config.ts](cci:7://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/altair/packages/altair-electron/electron.vite.config.ts:0:0-0:0))
- **Externalized Electron**: Marked `electron` as an external module in the main process Rollup configuration to ensure proper runtime bundling.
## Fixes
- Resolves #3024
## Validation
- [x] Verified that `pnpm compile` passes without errors.
- [x] Verified monorepo dependency linking (resolved `electron-vite` module resolution issues).
- [x] Verified that only necessary configuration files were modified.
---
*Note: During local development, pnpm was run using Node v20 to avoid compatibility crashes observed with v24.*

## Summary by Sourcery

Update Electron Snap and build configuration to resolve Ubuntu 24.04 startup issues and ensure correct Electron bundling.

Bug Fixes:
- Fix Snap startup failures on Ubuntu 24.04 by aligning the snap base and desktop integration with current Ubuntu requirements.

Enhancements:
- Update Snap configuration to use the core24 base, strict confinement, stable grade, and the gnome extension for desktop dependencies.
- Adjust Electron main process build to treat the electron module as external in the Rollup configuration.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Updated Linux Snap packaging to core24 with strict confinement and stable grade; removed a legacy system library layout binding.
  * Adjusted Electron build config to treat the Electron runtime as external, improving build correctness.
  * CI updates: conditional multi-arch Docker builds only when pushing; site deployments limited to master and release builds.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: feat: add in-sample forecasting support for reducers
prs.body:
## Description
This PR implements in-sample forecasting support for reducers ([_Reducer](cci:2://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/_reduce.py:218:0-582:19) base class and its descendants), addressing #2591.

Previously, reducers did not support generating forecasts for indices within the training set. This PR adds a vectorized [_predict_in_sample](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/base/_sktime.py:87:4-106:79) implementation that efficiently handles these requests by utilizing historical data (and exogenous variables) seen during training.

### Key Changes:
- **Vectorized In-Sample Logic**: Added [_predict_in_sample](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/base/_sktime.py:87:4-106:79) to [_Reducer](cci:2://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/_reduce.py:218:0-582:19) using sliding window logic for high performance.
- **Support for All Strategies**: Implemented logic for [recursive](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce.py:644:0-676:65), [direct](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce.py:644:0-676:65), and [multioutput](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/_reduce.py:2125:4-2174:19) reduction strategies.
- **Padding/Backfill**: Added automatic padding to handle windows at the very beginning of a series where insufficient history exists.
- **Bug Fix in [BaseForecaster](cci:2://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/base/_base.py:76:0-2701:65)**: Fixed a bug in [_check_fh](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/base/_base.py:2164:4-2302:17) where providing an in-sample horizon was incorrectly returning the fitted horizon (`self._fh`) instead of the newly requested horizon ([fh](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/base/_base.py:2151:4-2162:23)).
- **Updated Tags**: Set `capability:insample` and `capability:pred_int:insample` to `True` for the [_Reducer](cci:2://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/_reduce.py:218:0-582:19) class.

## How Has This Been Tested?
- **Regression Testing**: Verified that all 242 existing tests in [sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce.py](cci:7://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce.py:0:0-0:0) pass.
- **New Feature Tests**: Added [sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce_insample.py](cci:7://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/sktime/sktime/forecasting/compose/tests/test_reduce_insample.py:0:0-0:0) covering:
    - Consistency across recursive, direct, and multioutput strategies.
    - Correctness with exogenous variables (`X`).
    - Handling of extremely early forecasting horizons (indices 0, 1, etc.).

## Checklist
- [x] My code follows the style guidelines of this project
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [x] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [x] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes

prs.title: fix: resolve IndexError in decompress_list for empty input
prs.body:
## Fix: resolve IndexError in decompress_list for empty input
**Fixes #3017**
### Problem
The `decompress_list` function was not guarding against empty string input. Since `compress_list([])` legitimately returns an empty string `""`, calling `decompress_list(compress_list([]))` would immediately crash with:
`IndexError: string index out of range` at line 387 (`if lststr[0] == zlibmarker:`).
### Fix
Added an early return guard `if not lststr: return []` in `decompress_list`.
### Change
- File: `metaflow/util.py`
- Added check to return `[]` if input string is empty.
### Verification
Verified with a round-trip test:
`decompress_list(compress_list([]))` now correctly returns `[]` instead of raising an IndexError.

prs.title: fix: correct filename typo in timeout_decorator stack-frame filter
prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes #3016

## Problem
In [_sigalrm_handler](cci:1://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/metaflow/metaflow/plugins/timeout_decorator.py:80:4-97:9), the stack-frame filter was checking for `"timeout_decorators.py"` (with a trailing `s`) — a string that never matches the actual filename `"timeout_decorator.py"`. This made the filter **dead code**, so every frame — including Metaflow's internal decorator frames — was always included in the [TimeoutException](cci:2://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/metaflow/metaflow/plugins/timeout_decorator.py:9:0-10:25) message,
polluting user-facing tracebacks.

## Fix
Remove the spurious trailing `s` so the filename matches correctly.

## Change
**File:** [metaflow/plugins/timeout_decorator.py](cci:7://file:///Users/vanshagarwal/metaflow/metaflow/plugins/timeout_decorator.py:0:0-0:0), line 84

```diff
-    if "timeout_decorators.py" not in line:
+    if "timeout_decorator.py" not in line:

prs.title: fix: Normalize config dir env vars to absolute
prs.body:
# Normalize Config Dir Env Vars to Absolute Paths

## Overview
- This change ensures that config directory environment variables are normalised to absolute paths during process startup. It prevents “Path is not absolute” failures when these env vars are set to relative paths (common in containers or CI). The implementation is isolated to the shim. File touched: <mcfile name="mod.rs" path="/Users/vanshagarwal/turborepo/crates/turborepo-lib/src/shim/mod.rs"></mcfile>

## Affected Environment Variables
- `TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH`
- `VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH`

## Problem
- When either env var is set to a relative path (e.g., `./.config/turbo` inside a container), downstream code rejects it and surfaces a `NotAbsolute` error. This breaks builds and telemetry writes in setups that rely on relative paths.

## Root Cause
- The previous logic assumed env-provided paths were absolute and did not normalize relative ones.

## Solution
- On startup, the shim reads `TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH` and `VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH`.
- If a value is relative, it resolves it against the current working directory and canonicalizes it.
- If a value is already absolute or unset, it remains unchanged.

## How It Works (Summary)
1. Read env values.
2. Detect relative paths.
3. Convert relative → absolute using the current working directory.
4. Proceed with the resolved absolute path through the rest of the system.

## Usage Examples

### Set a relative config directory locally
```bash
export TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=./.config/turbo
export VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=./.config/vercel
```
- The shim will normalize these to absolute paths automatically.

### Set absolute paths (no change needed)
```bash
export TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=/home/user/project/.config/turbo
export VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=/home/user/project/.config/vercel
```

## Validation

### Local checks
- `cargo fmt` — formatting is clean
- `taplo format` — Cargo.toml formatting is clean
- `cargo test -p turborepo-dirs` — config resolution behavior verified
- `cargo test -p turborepo-telemetry` — telemetry behavior verified

### Targeted linting (modified crates only)
- `cargo clippy -p turborepo-lib -p turborepo-dirs --no-deps -- -D warnings`

### Container sanity checks (example)
- Set `TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH` and/or `VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH` to a relative path inside the container.
- Run tasks that read/write the config directory.
- Observe no `NotAbsolute` errors; paths resolve to absolute.

## Scope
- Only the shim implementation was changed: <mcfile name="mod.rs" path="/Users/vanshagarwal/turborepo/crates/turborepo-lib/src/shim/mod.rs"></mcfile>
- No public API changes.
- Backwards-compatible: unset or absolute env values are unaffected.

## Notes on Linting
- Workspace-wide clippy may report unrelated warnings in other crates (e.g., an unused `Color` import in UI and an unused `ShutdownFailed` struct in process). For a minimal PR, run targeted clippy on the modified crates as shown above. If desired, follow-up cleanups can address those warnings separately.

prs.title: added geolocation
prs.body:
## Summary
Add real-time geolocation to the Weather Dashboard:
- Auto-detects user location on first load and loads local weather
- Adds a “Use My Location” button to fetch weather via current coordinates

## Changes
- App.jsx: auto geolocation on mount; fetch weather by lat/lon (OpenWeather)
- components/SearchBar.jsx: new “Use My Location” button; fetch by lat/lon
- Handles permission denials and failures gracefully (no crashes; manual search still works)

## Motivation / Context
Improves first-run UX and reduces friction for users who just want local weather without typing a city. Feature request from discussion on enhancing the dashboard.

## How to Test
1) Ensure `VITE_OPEN_WEATHER_APP_KEY` is set in the weather app environment
2) Run: `cd React/weather-dashboard && npm install && npm run dev`
3) On first load, allow location permission; verify weather populates for your area
4) Click “Use My Location” to re-fetch and confirm weather updates
5) Deny permission and confirm app remains usable (manual search works, no errors)

prs.title: feat: use vsce Node API for packaging/publishing; update tests
prs.body:
Switch packaging and Visual Studio Marketplace publishing from vsce CLI invocations to the official vsce Node API. Keeps Open VSX publishing via CLI. Updates tests to mock the vsce API.
## **Changes**

1. Packaging

> - Use @vscode/vsce/out/api.js#createVSIX with IPackageOptions (cwd, version, out, optional target).

2. Publishing (VS Marketplace)

> - Use @vscode/vsce/out/api.js#publish with IPublishOptions (cwd, packagePath array or noGitTagVersion, optional target, optional azureCredential).

3. Publishing (Open VSX)

> - Remains via ovsx CLI.

2. Tests

> - Updated to mock @vscode/vsce/out/api.js and assert correct options.
> - Suite is green locally.

 ## **Rationale**

> - Avoids spawning child processes; uses stable programmatic API.
> - Prepares for more flexible option coverage (parity with CLI) and better error surfacing.

**## Backward Compatibility**
**Behavior is preserved:**

> - packageVsix and publish flows unchanged for callers.
> - Target handling with VSCE_TARGET remains.
> - VSCE_AZURE_CREDENTIAL supported via API.
> - Open VSX path unchanged.

- Types align with vsce API expectations; external plugin API remains the same.

## **Notes**

> - vsce packaging now takes IPackageOptions (replacing deprecated ICreateVSIXOptions internally).
> - vsce publishing uses IPublishOptions. Open VSX still uses CLI until we adopt its API in a follow‑up.

**## Testing**

- 59 tests passing; lints clean.

issues.title: [BUG] Message Bus Data Leakage across concurrent subscribers
issues.body:
### Operating System

macOS 26.4

### Python Version

3.14.3

### PocketPaw Version

0.4.15

### Installation Method

pip install pocketpaw

### Bug Description

### Description
A race condition exists in the `MessageBus` where concurrent subscribers receive shared mutable references to `OutboundMessage`'s `media` and `metadata` fields. If one subscriber modifies these fields (e.g., adding platform-specific metadata), other subscribers see the modified version, leading to cross-channel interference.

### Proposed Fix
1. Make `OutboundMessage` a `frozen` dataclass.
2. Use `copy.deepcopy` to isolate `metadata` and `media` for each subscriber during fanning out.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Register two subscribers to the `TELEGRAM` channel.
2. Publish an `OutboundMessage` with a metadata dictionary.
3. Have the first subscriber add a key to the metadata.
4. The second subscriber will find that key present in its own message instance. 

### Expected Behavior

Each subscriber to a message channel should receive an isolated copy of the message. Modifying mutable fields like `metadata` or `media` in one subscriber should NOT affect other subscribers or communication channels.

### Actual Behavior

Subscribers currently receive shared references to the same mutable objects. If one subscriber (e.g., a Telegram adapter) adds a platform-specific key to the `metadata` dictionary, that key is leaked to all other concurrent subscribers (e.g., a Discord adapter or a logging service).

### Terminal Output or Screenshot

FAILED tests/reproduce_bus_leak.py::test_outbound_message_data_leakage - AssertionError: Data leakage detected: Subscriber 2 saw Subscriber 1's modification!
FAILED tests/reproduce_bus_leak.py::test_broadcast_isolation - AssertionError: Data leakage detected in broadcast: Discord saw Telegram's modification!

### Relevant Files

src/pocketpaw/bus/events.py
src/pocketpaw/bus/queue.py

### Before submitting

- [x] I have installed and run PocketPaw locally
- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have included terminal output or a screenshot as evidence

issues.title: feat: add GitHub integration tool for repository and issue management
issues.body:
### Problem Statement

PocketPaw currently lacks a dedicated tool for interacting with the GitHub ecosystem. As an AI assistant for developers, being able to directly query repository information, list active issues, and retrieve detailed conversation context from pull requests is a critical missing capability.

### Proposed Solution

I've implemented a robust GitHubTool that provides the agent with high-level access to the GitHub REST API.

Available Actions:
get_repo: Fetches repository metadata (stars, forks, open issues count, description).
list_issues: Lists open issues and pull requests in a specific repository.
get_issue: Retrieves full content and the top/most recent comments for a specific issue or PR number.

### Files That Would Need Changes

- src/pocketpaw/credentials.py (Add 'github_api_token' to SECRET_FIELDS)
- src/pocketpaw/config.py (Add github_api_token to Settings)
- src/pocketpaw/integrations/github.py (NEW: GitHub Client)
- src/pocketpaw/tools/builtin/github.py (NEW: GitHub Tool implementation)
- src/pocketpaw/tools/builtin/__init__.py (Register Tool for lazy loading)
- tests/test_github_tool.py (NEW: Comprehensive unit tests)

### Alternatives Considered

_No response_

### Additional Context

_No response_

### Before submitting

- [x] I have explored the PocketPaw codebase and understand the relevant modules
- [x] I have searched existing issues and this is not a duplicate
- [x] I have identified the specific files this would affect

issues.title: Normalize relative config dir env vars to absolute at startup
issues.body:
Summary
Relative TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH / VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH (e.g. ./config) resolve against the package cwd during task execution, causing wrong or missing config. Normalizing to absolute at startup keeps resolution stable across packages and --cwd.

Repro
1) Monorepo with apps/web and config at repo root.
2) Set TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=./config (and optionally VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH=./config).
3) Run turbo run build --filter=@acme/web.
Actual: resolves to apps/web/config. Expected: <repo>/config.

References
- crates/turborepo-dirs/src/lib.rs:15-33 handles TURBO_CONFIG_DIR_PATH normalization.
- crates/turborepo-dirs/src/lib.rs:46-53,157-166 currently require VERCEL_CONFIG_DIR_PATH to be absolute.

Proposed fix
Normalize both env vars to absolute at startup.

Related PR
https://github.com/vercel/turborepo/pull/11146
